Requires the remoted debugging server to be started in the Chrome instance via chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging.  [boolean] [default: false]\n  -u, --browserUrl              Connect to a running, debuggable Chrome instance (e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:9222`). For more details see: https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp#connecting-to-a-running-chrome-instance.  [string]\n  -w, --wsEndpoint              WebSocket endpoint to connect to a running Chrome instance (e.g., ws://127.0.0.1:9222/devtools/browser/<id>). Alternative to --browserUrl.  [string]\n      --wsHeaders               Custom headers for WebSocket connection in JSON format (e.g., '{\"Authorization\":\"Bearer token\"}'). Only works with --wsEndpoint.  [string]\n      --headless                Whether to run in headless (no UI) mode.  [boolean] [default: false]\n  -e, --executablePath          Path to custom Chrome executable.  [string]\n      --isolated                If specified, creates a temporary user-data-dir that is automatically cleaned up after the browser is closed. Defaults to false.  [boolean]\n      --userDataDir             Path to the user data directory for Chrome. Default is $HOME/.cache/chrome-devtools-mcp/chrome-profile$CHANNEL_SUFFIX_IF_NON_STABLE  [string]\n      --channel                 Specify a different Chrome channel that should be used. The default is the stable channel version.  [string] [choices: \"stable\", \"canary\", \"beta\", \"dev\"]\n      --logFile                 Path to a file to write debug logs to. Set the env variable `DEBUG` to `*` to enable verbose logs. Useful for submitting bug reports.  [string]\n      --viewport                Initial viewport size for the Chrome instances started by the server. For example, `1280x720`. In headless mode, max size is 3840x2160px.  [string]\n      --proxyServer             Proxy server configuration for Chrome passed as --proxy-server when launching the browser. See https://www.chromium.org/developers/design-documents/network-settings/ for details.  [string]\n      --acceptInsecureCerts     If enabled, ignores errors relative to self-signed and expired certificates. Use with caution.  [boolean]\n      --experimentalScreencast  Exposes experimental screencast tools (requires ffmpeg). Install ffmpeg https://www.ffmpeg.org/download.html and ensure it is available in the MCP server PATH.  [boolean]\n      --chromeArg               Additional arguments for Chrome. Only applies when Chrome is launched by chrome-devtools-mcp.  [array]\n      --ignoreDefaultChromeArg  Explicitly disable default arguments for Chrome. Only applies when Chrome is launched by chrome-devtools-mcp.  [array]\n      --categoryEmulation       Set to false to exclude tools related to emulation.  [boolean] [default: true]\n      --categoryPerformance     Set to false to exclude tools related to performance. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NFdGweyAtdsHy56Xz9WuUh","type":"tool_result","content":"<h1 align=\"center\">chrome-devtools-axi</h1>\n\n<p align=\"center\">\n  <a href=\"https://www.npmjs.com/package/chrome-devtools-axi\"><img alt=\"npm\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/npm/v/chrome-devtools-axi?style=flat-square\" /></a>\n  <a src=\"https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/kunchenguid/chrome-devtools-axi/release-please.yml?style=flat-square&label=Release\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"#\"><img alt=\"Platform\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-blue?style=flat-square\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"https://x.com/kunchenguid\"><img alt=\"X\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/X-@kunchenguid-black?style=flat-square\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"https://discord.gg/Wsy2NpnZDu\"><img alt=\"Discord\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/discord/1439901831038763092?style=flat-square&label=discord\" /></a>\n</p>\n\n<h3 align=\"center\">The most agent-ergonomic browser automation</h3>\n\n`chrome-devtools-axi` wraps [chrome-devtools-mcp](https://www.npmjs.com/package/chrome-devtools-mcp) with an [AXI](https://axi.md)-compliant CLI.\n\n- **Token-efficient** — TOON-encoded output cuts token usage ~40% vs raw JSON\n- **Combined operations** — one command navigates, captures, and suggests next steps\n- **Contextual suggestions** — every response includes actionable next-step hints\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```sh\n$ chrome-devtools-axi open https://example.com\npage: {title: \"Example Domain\", url: \"https://example.com\", refs: 1}\nsnapshot:\nRootWebArea \"Example Domain\"\n  heading \"Example Domain\"\n  paragraph \"This domain is for use in illustrative examples...\"\n  uid=1 link \"More information...\"\nhelp[1]:\n  Run `chrome-devtools-axi click @1` to click the \"More information...\" link\n\n$ chrome-devtools-axi click @1\npage: {title: \"IANA — IANA-Managed Reserved Domains\", refs: 12}\nsnapshot:\n...\n```\n\n## Install\n\n**Tell your agent:**\n\n```\nExecute `npx -y chrome-devtools-axi` to get browser automation tools.\n```\n\n## How It Works\n\n```\n┌───────────────────────┐\n│  chrome-devtools-axi  │  CLI — parse args, format output\n└──────────┬────────────┘\n           │ HTTP (localhost:9224)\n           ▼\n┌───────────────────────┐\n│     Bridge Server     │  Persistent process, manages MCP session\n└──────────┬────────────┘\n           │ stdio\n           ▼\n┌───────────────────────┐\n│  chrome-devtools-mcp  │  Headless Chrome via DevTools Protocol\n└───────────────────────┘\n```\n\n- **Persistent bridge** — a detached process keeps the MCP session alive across commands, so Chrome doesn't restart every invocation\n- **Auto-lifecycle** — the bridge starts on first command and writes a PID file to `~/.chrome-devtools-axi/bridge.pid`\n- **Snapshot parsing** — accessibility tree snapshots are extracted and analyzed for interactive elements (`uid=` refs)\n- **TOON encoding** — structured metadata uses [TOON format](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@toon-format/toon) for compact, token-efficient output\n\n## CLI Reference\n\n### Navigation\n\n| Command           | Description                                  |\n| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------- |\n| `open <url>`      | Navigate to URL and snapshot                 |\n| `snapshot`        | Capture current page state                   |\n| `screenshot <p>`  | Save a screenshot to a file                  |\n| `scroll <dir>`    | Scroll: up, down, top, bottom                |\n| `back`            | Navigate back                                |","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bd111f6e-964d-4b08-84ec-c5637d341ee6","uuid":"0cc2eea0-530b-405c-96c5-93cc71e263d1","timestamp":"2026-04-04T05:51:41.958Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"<h1 align=\"center\">chrome-devtools-axi</h1>\n\n<p align=\"center\">\n  <a href=\"https://www.npmjs.com/package/chrome-devtools-axi\"><img alt=\"npm\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/npm/v/chrome-devtools-axi?style=flat-square\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"https://github.com/kunchenguid/chrome-devtools-axi/actions/workflows/ci.yml\"><img alt=\"CI\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/kunchenguid/chrome-devtools-axi/ci.yml?style=flat-square&label=CI\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"https://github.com/kunchenguid/chrome-devtools-axi/actions/workflows/release-please.yml\"><img alt=\"Release\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/kunchenguid/chrome-devtools-axi/release-please.yml?style=flat-square&label=Release\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"#\"><img alt=\"Platform\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-blue?style=flat-square\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"https://x.com/kunchenguid\"><img alt=\"X\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/X-@kunchenguid-black?style=flat-square\" /></a>\n  <a href=\"https://discord.gg/Wsy2NpnZDu\"><img alt=\"Discord\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/discord/1439901831038763092?style=flat-square&label=discord\" /></a>\n</p>\n\n<h3 align=\"center\">The most agent-ergonomic browser automation</h3>\n\n`chrome-devtools-axi` wraps [chrome-devtools-mcp](https://www.npmjs.com/package/chrome-devtools-mcp) with an [AXI](https://axi.md)-compliant CLI.\n\n- **Token-efficient** — TOON-encoded output cuts token usage ~40% vs raw JSON\n- **Combined operations** — one command navigates, captures, and suggests next steps\n- **Contextual suggestions** — every response includes actionable next-step hints\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```sh\n$ chrome-devtools-axi open https://example.com\npage: {title: \"Example Domain\", url: \"https://example.com\", refs: 1}\nsnapshot:\nRootWebArea \"Example Domain\"\n  heading \"Example Domain\"\n  paragraph \"This domain is for use in illustrative examples...\"\n  uid=1 link \"More information...\"\nhelp[1]:\n  Run `chrome-devtools-axi click @1` to click the \"More information...\" link\n\n$ chrome-devtools-axi click @1\npage: {title: \"IANA — IANA-Managed Reserved Domains\", refs: 12}\nsnapshot:\n...\n```\n\n## Install\n\n**Tell your agent:**\n\n```\nExecute `npx -y chrome-devtools-axi` to get browser automation tools.\n```\n\n## How It Works\n\n```\n┌───────────────────────┐\n│  chrome-devtools-axi  │  CLI — parse args, format output\n└──────────┬────────────┘\n           │ HTTP (localhost:9224)\n           ▼\n┌───────────────────────┐\n│     Bridge Server     │  Persistent process, manages MCP session\n└──────────┬────────────┘\n           │ stdio\n           ▼\n┌───────────────────────┐\n│  chrome-devtools-mcp  │  Headless Chrome via DevTools Protocol\n└───────────────────────┘\n```\n\n- **Persistent bridge** — a detached process keeps the MCP session alive across commands, so Chrome doesn't restart every invocation\n- **Auto-lifecycle** — the bridge starts on first command and writes a PID file to `~/.chrome-devtools-axi/bridge.pid`\n- **Snapshot parsing** — accessibility tree snapshots are extracted and analyzed for interactive elements (`uid=` refs)\n- **TOON encoding** — structured metadata uses [TOON format](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@toon-format/toon) for compact, token-efficient output\n\n## CLI Reference\n\n### Navigation\n\n| Command           | Description                                  |\n| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------- |\n| `open <url>`      | Navigate to URL and snapshot                 |\n| `snapshot`        | Capture current page state                   |\n| `screenshot <p>`  | Save a screenshot to a file                  |\n| `scroll <dir>`    | Scroll: up, down, top, bottom                |\n| `back`            | Navigate back                                |","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01FeSAWdY6fYrixdtAN8qgix","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01M8nq8FJfJvm6eYCGBkorWs","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat /Users/kunchen/.nvm/versions/node/v24.13.1/lib/node_modules/chrome-devtools-axi/dist/src/bridge.js 2>&1 | head -100","description":"Check bridge.js to understand startup mechanism"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":192,"cache_read_input_tokens":21059,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":192},"output_tokens":67,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bd111f6e-964d-4b08-84ec-c5637d341ee6","uuid":"402bd64b-cee3-40f6-bcc0-e84c6b5861a2"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01M8nq8FJfJvm6eYCGBkorWs","type":"tool_result","content":"/**\n * Persistent MCP bridge server for chrome-devtools-axi.\n *\n * Spawns chrome-devtools-mcp as a child process and maintains a single\n * persistent MCP session. Exposes a simple HTTP API:\n *   POST /call  { name, args }  → { result }\n *   GET  /tools                 → [{ name, description }]\n *   GET  /health                → { status: \"ok\" }\n *\n * Writes a PID file to ~/.chrome-devtools-axi/bridge.pid on startup.\n */\nimport { Client } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js\";\nimport { StdioClientTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js\";\nimport { createServer, } from \"node:http\";\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, unlinkSync, writeFileSync } from \"node:fs\";\nimport { join, resolve } from \"node:path\";\nimport { homedir } from \"node:os\";\nconst DEFAULT_PORT = Number.parseInt(process.env.CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_PORT ?? \"9224\", 10);\nconst STATE_DIR = join(homedir(), \".chrome-devtools-axi\");\nconst PID_FILE = join(STATE_DIR, \"bridge.pid\");\nexport async function isBridgeClientConnected(client) {\n    try {\n        await client.listTools();\n        return true;\n    }\n    catch {\n        return false;\n    }\n}\nfunction writePidFile(port) {\n    mkdirSync(STATE_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    writeFileSync(PID_FILE, JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, port }));\n}\nfunction removePidFile() {\n    try {\n        unlinkSync(PID_FILE);\n    }\n    catch {\n        // Already gone — fine\n    }\n}\nexport function getErrorMessage(error) {\n    return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);\n}\nexport function extractToolText(content) {\n    return content\n        .filter((block) => block.type === \"text\" && typeof block.text === \"string\")\n        .map((block) => block.text)\n        .join(\"\\n\");\n}\nfunction getToolContent(result) {\n    if (!result ||\n        typeof result !== \"object\" ||\n        !(\"content\" in result) ||\n        !Array.isArray(result.content)) {\n        return [];\n    }\n    return result.content;\n}\nexport function parseBridgeCallPayload(body) {\n    let payload;\n    try {\n        payload = JSON.parse(body);\n    }\n    catch {\n        throw new Error(\"Invalid bridge request payload\");\n    }\n    if (typeof payload.name !== \"string\" || payload.name.length === 0) {\n        throw new Error(\"Invalid bridge request payload\");\n    }\n    if (payload.args === undefined) {\n        return { name: payload.name, args: {} };\n    }\n    if (payload.args === null ||\n        typeof payload.args !== \"object\" ||\n        Array.isArray(payload.args)) {\n        throw new Error(\"Invalid bridge request payload\");\n    }\n    return { name: payload.name, args: payload.args };\n}\nexport function resolveBridgeScript(importMetaDir) {\n    const builtScript = resolve(importMetaDir, \"../bin/chrome-devtools-axi-bridge.js\");\n    const sourceScript = builtScript.replace(/\\.js$/, \".ts\");\n    return existsSync(sourceScript) ? sourceScript : builtScript;\n}\nasync function readRequestBody(req) {\n    let body = \"\";\n    for await (const chunk of req) {\n        body += typeof chunk === \"string\" ? chunk : chunk.toString(\"utf-8\");\n    }\n    return body;\n}\nfunction writeJson(res, statusCode, payload) {\n    res.statusCode = statusCode;\n    res.end(JSON.stringify(payload));\n}\nasync function handleToolsRequest(client, res) {\n    const result = await client.listTools();\n    writeJson(res, 200, result.tools.map((tool) => ({\n        name: tool.name,","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bd111f6e-964d-4b08-84ec-c5637d341ee6","uuid":"aa975aca-e61a-4c79-abcb-d4a9d1fabb4c","timestamp":"2026-04-04T05:52:59.659Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"/**\n * Persistent MCP bridge server for chrome-devtools-axi.\n *\n * Spawns chrome-devtools-mcp as a child process and maintains a single\n * persistent MCP session. Exposes a simple HTTP API:\n *   POST /call  { name, args }  → { result }\n *   GET  /tools                 → [{ name, description }]\n *   GET  /health                → { status: \"ok\" }\n *\n * Writes a PID file to ~/.chrome-devtools-axi/bridge.pid on startup.\n */\nimport { Client } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js\";\nimport { StdioClientTransport } from \"@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js\";\nimport { createServer, } from \"node:http\";\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, unlinkSync, writeFileSync } from \"node:fs\";\nimport { join, resolve } from \"node:path\";\nimport { homedir } from \"node:os\";\nconst DEFAULT_PORT = Number.parseInt(process.env.CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_PORT ?? \"9224\", 10);\nconst STATE_DIR = join(homedir(), \".chrome-devtools-axi\");\nconst PID_FILE = join(STATE_DIR, \"bridge.pid\");\nexport async function isBridgeClientConnected(client) {\n    try {\n        await client.listTools();\n        return true;\n    }\n    catch {\n        return false;\n    }\n}\nfunction writePidFile(port) {\n    mkdirSync(STATE_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    writeFileSync(PID_FILE, JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, port }));\n}\nfunction removePidFile() {\n    try {\n        unlinkSync(PID_FILE);\n    }\n    catch {\n        // Already gone — fine\n    }\n}\nexport function getErrorMessage(error) {\n    return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);\n}\nexport function extractToolText(content) {\n    return content\n        .filter((block) => block.type === \"text\" && typeof block.text === \"string\")\n        .map((block) => block.text)\n        .join(\"\\n\");\n}\nfunction getToolContent(result) {\n    if (!result ||\n        typeof result !== \"object\" ||\n        !(\"content\" in result) ||\n        !Array.isArray(result.content)) {\n        return [];\n    }\n    return result.content;\n}\nexport function parseBridgeCallPayload(body) {\n    let payload;\n    try {\n        payload = JSON.parse(body);\n    }\n    catch {\n        throw new Error(\"Invalid bridge request payload\");\n    }\n    if (typeof payload.name !== \"string\" || payload.name.length === 0) {\n        throw new Error(\"Invalid bridge request payload\");\n    }\n    if (payload.args === undefined) {\n        return { name: payload.name, args: {} };\n    }\n    if (payload.args === null ||\n        typeof payload.args !== \"object\" ||\n        Array.isArray(payload.args)) {\n        throw new Error(\"Invalid bridge request payload\");\n    }\n    return { name: payload.name, args: payload.args };\n}\nexport function resolveBridgeScript(importMetaDir) {\n    const builtScript = resolve(importMetaDir, \"../bin/chrome-devtools-axi-bridge.js\");\n    const sourceScript = builtScript.replace(/\\.js$/, \".ts\");\n    return existsSync(sourceScript) ? sourceScript : builtScript;\n}\nasync function readRequestBody(req) {\n    let body = \"\";\n    for await (const chunk of req) {\n        body += typeof chunk === \"string\" ? chunk : chunk.toString(\"utf-8\");\n    }\n    return body;\n}\nfunction writeJson(res, statusCode, payload) {\n    res.statusCode = statusCode;\n    res.end(JSON.stringify(payload));\n}\nasync function handleToolsRequest(client, res) {\n    const result = await client.listTools();\n    writeJson(res, 200, result.tools.map((tool) => ({\n        name: tool.name,","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

\".chrome-devtools-axi\");\nconst PID_FILE = join(STATE_DIR, \"bridge.pid\");\nconst DEFAULT_PORT = 9224;\nexport class CdpError extends AxiError {\n    code;\n    suggestions;\n    constructor(message, code, suggestions = []) {\n        super(message, code, suggestions);\n        this.code = code;\n        this.suggestions = suggestions;\n        this.name = \"CdpError\";\n    }\n}\nfunction readPidFile() {\n    try {\n        if (!existsSync(PID_FILE))\n            return null;\n        const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(PID_FILE, \"utf-8\"));\n        if (typeof data.pid === \"number\" && typeof data.port === \"number\") {\n            return data;\n        }\n        return null;\n    }\n    catch {\n        return null;\n    }\n}\nfunction isProcessAlive(pid) {\n    try {\n        process.kill(pid, 0);\n        return true;\n    }\n    catch {\n        return false;\n    }\n}\nfunction httpGet(port, path, timeoutMs = 2000) {\n    return reject) => {\n        const payload = JSON.stringify(body);\n        const req = request({\n            hostname: \"127.0.0.1\",\n            port,\n            path,\n            method: \"POST\",\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KR7M5JZuMyS61Ex2wpfVJ6","type":"tool_result","content":"    throw new CdpError(\"Bridge failed to start within 30s\", \"BRIDGE_NOT_READY\", [\n        \"Check that chrome-devtools-mcp is installed: npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --help\",\n    ]);\n}\n/**\n * Call an MCP tool via the bridge. Returns the text result.\n */\nexport async function callTool(name, args = {}) {\n    const port = await ensureBridge();\n    try {\n        const resp = await httpPost(port, \"/call\", { name, args (message.includes(\"ECONNREFUSED\") || message.includes(\"ECONNRESET\")) {\n        return new CdpError(\"Bridge is not running\", \"BRIDGE_NOT_READY\", [\n            \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi open <url>` — the bridge starts automatically\",\n        ]);\n    }\n    if ((message.includes(\"uid\") || message.includes(\"element\")) &&\n        (message.includes(\"not found\") || message.includes(\"invalid\"))) {\n        return new CdpError(message, \"REF_NOT_FOUND\", [\n            \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi snapshot` to see available elements and their @uid refs\",\n        ]);\n    }\n    if (message.includes(\"timeout\") || message.includes(\"timed out\")) {\n        return new CdpError(message, \"TIMEOUT\", [\n            \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi snapshot` to see current page state\",\n        ]);\n    }\n    // Try to parse JSON error\n    try {\n        const parsed = JSON.parse(message);\n        if (parsed.error) {\n            return new CdpError(parsed.error, \"BROWSER_ERROR\", [\n                \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi snapshot` to see current page state\",\n            ]);\n        }\n    }\n    catch {\n        // Not JSON\n    }\n    return new CdpError(message, \"UNKNOWN\");\n}\n/**\n * Get the current page snapshot without starting the bridge.\n * Returns null if the bridge is not running or healthy.\n */\nexport async \"\";\n    }\n    catch (err) {\n        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n        throw mapErrorMessage(message);\n    }\n}\nexport function mapErrorMessage(message) {\n    if (message.includes(\"ECONNREFUSED\") || message.includes(\"ECONNRESET\")) {\n        return new CdpError(\"Bridge is not running\", \"BRIDGE_NOT_READY\", [\n            \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi open <url>` — the bridge starts automatically\",\n        ]);\n    }\n    if ((message.includes(\"uid\") || message.includes(\"element\")) &&\n        (message.includes(\"not found\") || message.includes(\"invalid\"))) {\n        return new CdpError(message, \"REF_NOT_FOUND\", [\n            \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi snapshot` to see available elements and their @uid refs\",\n        ]);\n    }\n    if (message.includes(\"timeout\") || message.includes(\"timed out\")) {\n        return new CdpError(message, \"TIMEOUT\", [\n            \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi snapshot` to see current page state\",\n        ]);\n    }\n    // Try to parse JSON error\n    try {\n        const parsed = JSON.parse(message);\n        if (parsed.error) {\n            return new CdpError(parsed.error, \"BROWSER_ERROR\", [\n                \"Run `chrome-devtools-axi snapshot` to see current page state\",\n            ]);\n        }\n    }\n    catch {\n        // Not JSON\n    }\n    return new CdpError(message, \"UNKNOWN\");\n}\n/**\n * Get the current page snapshot without starting the bridge.\n * Returns null if the bridge is not running or healthy.\n */\nexport async function getSessionSnapshotIfRunning() {\n    const pidInfo = readPidFile();\n    if (!pidInfo || !isProcessAlive(pidInfo.pid)) {\n        return null;\n    }\n    if (!(await checkBridgeHealth(pidInfo.port))) {\n        return null;\n    }","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KQHJYCwTmxDGSZETKkux7A","type":"tool_result","content":"    return new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));\n}\n/**\n * Ensure the bridge is running, starting it if needed. Returns the port.\n */\nexport async function ensureBridge() {\n    const port = parseInt(process.env.CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_PORT ?? String(DEFAULT_PORT), 10);\n    // Check existing bridge via PID file\n    const pidInfo = readPidFile();\n    if (pidInfo && isProcessAlive(pidInfo.pid)) {\n        if (await checkBridgeHealth(pidInfo.port)) {\n            return pidInfo.port;\n        }\n        try {\n            process.kill(pidInfo.pid, \"SIGTERM\");\n        }\n        catch {\n            // Best effort — if shutdown fails, the startup poll below will time out.\n        }\n    }\n    // Start a new bridge\n    const bridgeScript = resolveBridgeScript(import.meta.dirname);\n    // Try .ts first (dev mode), fall back to .js (built)\n    const script = existsSync(bridgeScript.replace(/\\.js$/, \".ts\"))\n        ? bridgeScript.replace(/\\.js$/, \".ts\")\n        : bridgeScript;\n    const runner = script.endsWith(\".ts\") ? \"tsx\" : \"node\";\n    const child = spawn(runner === \"tsx\" ? \"npx\" : \"node\", runner === \"tsx\" ? [\"tsx\", script] : [script], {\n        stdio: \"ignore\",\n        env: { ...process.env, CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_PORT: String(port) },\n        detached: true,\n    });\n    child.unref();\n    // Poll for health (max 30s — Chrome launch can be slow)\n    const deadline = Date.now() + 30_000;\n    while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n        if (await checkBridgeHealth(port)) {\n            return port;\n        }\n        await sleep(500);\n    }\n    throw new CdpError(\"Bridge failed to start within 30s\", \"BRIDGE_NOT_READY\", [\n        \"Check that chrome-devtools-mcp is installed: npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --help\",\n    ]);\n}\n/**\n * Call an MCP tool via the bridge. Returns the text result.\n */\nexport async function callTool(name, args = {}) {\n    const port = await ensureBridge();\n    try {\n        const resp = await httpPost(port, \"/call\", { name, args });\n        const data = JSON.parse(resp);\n        if (data.error) {\n            throw new Error(data.error);\n        }","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bd111f6e-964d-4b08-84ec-c5637d341ee6","uuid":"dcefdb78-3160-4c8f-9351-21ad29ad903b","timestamp":"2026-04-04T05:53:06.897Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"    return new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));\n}\n/**\n * Ensure the bridge is running, starting it if needed. Returns the port.\n */\nexport async function ensureBridge() {\n    const port = parseInt(process.env.CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_PORT ?? String(DEFAULT_PORT), 10);\n    // Check existing bridge via PID file\n    const pidInfo = readPidFile();\n    if (pidInfo && isProcessAlive(pidInfo.pid)) {\n        if (await checkBridgeHealth(pidInfo.port)) {\n            return pidInfo.port;\n        }\n        try {\n            process.kill(pidInfo.pid, \"SIGTERM\");\n        }\n        catch {\n            // Best effort — if shutdown fails, the startup poll below will time out.\n        }\n    }\n    // Start a new bridge\n    const bridgeScript = resolveBridgeScript(import.meta.dirname);\n    // Try .ts first (dev mode), fall back to .js (built)\n    const script = existsSync(bridgeScript.replace(/\\.js$/, \".ts\"))\n        ? bridgeScript.replace(/\\.js$/, \".ts\")\n        : bridgeScript;\n    const runner = script.endsWith(\".ts\") ? \"tsx\" : \"node\";\n    const child = spawn(runner === \"tsx\" ? \"npx\" : \"node\", runner === \"tsx\" ? [\"tsx\", script] : [script], {\n        stdio: \"ignore\",\n        env: { ...process.env, CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_PORT: String(port) },\n        detached: true,\n    });\n    child.unref();\n    // Poll for health (max 30s — Chrome launch can be slow)\n    const deadline = Date.now() + 30_000;\n    while (Date.now() < deadline) {\n        if (await checkBridgeHealth(port)) {\n            return port;\n        }\n        await sleep(500);\n    }\n    throw new CdpError(\"Bridge failed to start within 30s\", \"BRIDGE_NOT_READY\", [\n        \"Check that chrome-devtools-mcp is installed: npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest --help\",\n    ]);\n}\n/**\n * Call an MCP tool via the bridge. Returns the text result.\n */\nexport async function callTool(name, args = {}) {\n    const port = await ensureBridge();\n    try {\n        const resp = await httpPost(port, \"/call\", { name, args });\n        const data = JSON.parse(resp);\n        if (data.error) {\n            throw new Error(data.error);\n        }","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
